docs.title: Leanmote Documentation
docs.text:
Leanmote helps engineering organizations improve delivery performance with shared visibility across code, planning, and collaboration tools.

This documentation is focused on users of app.leanmote.com: connecting integrations, reading metrics, and taking action from dashboards.

docs.title: Why teams use Leanmote
docs.text:
• Improve delivery predictability with DORA and flow metrics
  • Detect bottlenecks before they become delivery risks
  • Align engineering managers and executives around one metrics source
  • Turn operational data into weekly decisions, not monthly surprises

docs.title: What you can do in the platform
docs.text:
• Connect your engineering tools (GitHub, GitLab, Jira, Slack, Azure DevOps, and others)
  • Build dashboards for teams, departments, and executive reporting
  • Analyze engineering health by team, repository, workflow, or date range
  • Track trends for delivery speed, quality, throughput, and collaboration

docs.title: Suggested reading path
docs.text:
1. Getting Started Overview
  2. Quick Start
  3. Using app.leanmote.com
  4. Metrics Methodology
  5. API Overview

docs.title: Delivery performance
docs.text: Leanmote gives leaders an objective view of deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, and recovery speed.

docs.title: Flow efficiency
docs.text: You can monitor throughput, cycle time, and work in progress to identify where work stalls.

docs.title: Team collaboration
docs.text: Leanmote highlights review participation, interaction patterns, and execution consistency across teams.

docs.title: Need implementation details?
docs.text: This docs site prioritizes public, product-facing guidance. Internal implementation details are intentionally excluded from the main navigation.

docs.title: Leanmote Architecture Analysis
docs.text: Comprehensive technical analysis of the Leanmote platform architecture, components, data models, and integration points.

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text:
Leanmote is a multi-tenant SaaS platform that aggregates engineering metrics from 20+ development tools. The architecture follows a hybrid serverless pattern with:

  • Frontend: CodeIgniter 4 PHP web application (Elastic Beanstalk)
  • Backend: AWS Lambda functions for API, data extraction, and sync
  • Data Storage: MySQL (RDS) for relational data, Neo4j Aura for graph analytics
  • AI Pipeline: LangGraph-based multi-agent system for insight generation

docs.title: 1. leanmote (Web Application)
docs.text:
Path: C:\Users\Usuario\Desktop\Leanmote\leanmote Framework: CodeIgniter 4 (PHP 8.1+) Deployment: AWS Elastic Beanstalk

Purpose: Main user-facing application providing dashboards, integration management, and team administration.

docs.title: 2. lambda (Serverless Functions)
docs.text:
Path: C:\Users\Usuario\Desktop\Leanmote\lambda Runtime: Python 3.11+ Deployment: AWS Lambda

Purpose: API services, data extraction, synchronization, and telemetry processing.

docs.title: 3. insight_agent (AI Pipeline)
docs.text:
Path: C:\Users\Usuario\Desktop\Leanmote\insight_agent Framework: LangGraph + LangChain Runtime: Python 3.11+

Purpose: AI-powered research, problem discovery, and Cypher query generation.

docs.text: Platform OAuth Webhooks API Pull Data Types GitHub Yes Yes Yes Repos, commits, PRs, reviews GitLab Yes Yes Yes Projects, MRs, discussions Bitbucket Yes No Yes Repos, commits, PRs Jira Yes Yes Yes Projects, issues, sprints Linear Yes Yes Yes Teams, issues, cycles Slack Yes Yes Yes Channels, messages Discord Yes No Yes Servers, channels, messages Azure DevOps Yes Yes Yes Repos, boards, work items ClickUp Yes Yes Yes Spaces, lists, tasks Monday.com Yes Yes Yes Boards, items Notion Yes Yes Yes Databases, pages Google Calendar Yes No Yes Events, availability

docs.title: OAuth Integration Flow
docs.text:
1. User initiates connection → /oauth/start
2. Redirect to provider OAuth consent
3. Callback with auth code → /oauth/callback
4. Exchange code for tokens
5. Store tokens in user_apps_credentials
6. Initial data sync triggered

docs.title: Cron-based Data Sync
docs.text:
1. Cron job picks from cron_queue
2. Fetch data via provider API
3. Transform and normalize data
4. Store in respective tables
5. Record interactions in user_interactions
6. Update last_update timestamp

docs.title: Real-time Webhook Processing
docs.text:
1. Receive webhook payload
2. Validate signature
3. Parse event type
4. Update relevant entities
5. Enqueue follow-up tasks if needed

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
• Web Application: AWS Cognito with magic link support
  • API: Token-based (SHA256 hashed, stored in api_tokens)
  • OAuth: Per-platform OAuth 2.0 flows

docs.title: Authorization
docs.text:
• Role-based: Admin (1), Manager (2), Collaborator (3)
  • Organization-scoped: All queries filtered by organization
  • Team-scoped: Optional team-level filtering

docs.title: Token Security
docs.text:
Token Generation:
1. Generate 36-char UUID
2. Store SHA256(token + salt) as token_hash
3. Store first 5 chars as token_preview
4. Return full token to user (once)

Token Validation:
1. Receive token in Authorization header
2. Compute SHA256(token + salt)
3. Query api_tokens by hash
4. Validate is_active and expires_at
5. Update last_used_at

docs.title: Current Architecture
docs.text:
• Horizontal scaling: Elastic Beanstalk auto-scaling
  • Serverless: Lambda scales automatically
  • Database: Single RDS instance (vertical scaling)
  • Graph: Neo4j Aura (managed scaling)

docs.title: Bottlenecks & Solutions
docs.text: Component Bottleneck Solution MySQL Single instance Read replicas, Aurora Cron jobs Sequential processing Distributed queue (SQS) Large orgs Data volume Partitioning by date API calls Rate limits Per-provider throttling

docs.title: Error Tracking
docs.text:
• Sentry: PHP application errors
  • CloudWatch: Lambda function logs

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
• cron_log table: Execution history
  • metrics table: Application metrics
  • user_logs table: User activity

docs.title: Distributed Tracing
docs.text:
• trace_id in SQS messages
  • Process tracking via cron_queue

docs.title: Python (Insight Agent)
docs.text:
langgraph==0.2.75
langchain-core==0.3.51
langchain-openai==0.3.11
langchain-google-genai==2.1.4
fastapi==0.115.6
sqlalchemy==2.0.35

docs.title: 1. Multi-tenant by Organization
docs.text: All data is scoped to id_organization, enabling strict data isolation.

docs.title: 2. Hybrid Serverless
docs.text: Web app on Elastic Beanstalk for stateful operations, Lambda for stateless API and processing.

docs.title: 3. Separate Graph Database
docs.text: Neo4j for complex relationship queries (bottleneck detection, collaboration patterns).

docs.title: 4. Event-driven Data Sync
docs.text: Webhooks for real-time updates, cron for batch processing.

docs.title: 5. AI Pipeline Isolation
docs.text: Insight Agent runs independently, persisting results to MySQL.

docs.title: Future Architecture Considerations
docs.text:
1. Event Sourcing: Full audit trail of all changes
  2. CQRS: Separate read/write paths for dashboards
  3. GraphQL: Flexible querying for dashboard customization
  4. Kubernetes: Container orchestration for compute-heavy workloads
  5. Data Lake: Historical data archival and advanced analytics

docs.title: Insight Agent (LangGraph)
docs.text: Multi-agent pipeline for investigating software development problems, generating Cypher queries, and simulating execution with human-in-the-loop validation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Insight Agent is a sophisticated LangGraph-based system that automatically discovers software development inefficiencies, validates findings with humans, and generates Neo4j Cypher queries to measure them.

Tech Stack: - LangGraph 0.2.75 - Pipeline orchestration - LangChain 0.3.x - LLM integrations (OpenAI GPT-5.2, Gemini 3) - FastAPI 0.115.6 - Web UI backend - SQLAlchemy 2.0.35 - Database ORM (MySQL) - Perplexity API - Search discovery - Gemini API - Web-enabled research with grounding

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
User Input (Topic)
    │
    ▼
Research Agent
  ├─ Perplexity Search (6 queries)
  └─ Gemini URL Context Analysis
    │
    ▼
Problem Validator (Human-in-the-loop)
  ├─ Check sources
  └─ Human approval
    │
    ▼
Feasibility Analyzer
  ├─ Check against Neo4j schema
  └─ Gap analysis
    │
    ▼
Query Generator
  ├─ Build Cypher template
  └─ Add parameters
    │
    ▼
Human Validator
  └─ Approve/reject Cypher
    │
    ▼
Query Executor
  ├─ Simulate (default)
  └─ Return sensors
    │
    ▼
Database (Optional)
  └─ Persist insight + tool

docs.text:
insight_agent/
├── src/
│   ├── agents/           # Agent implementations
│   │   ├── research_agent.py
│   │   ├── problem_validation.py
│   │   ├── feasibility_analyzer.py
│   │   ├── query_generator.py

│   │   ├── query_executor.py
│   │   └── prompt_generator.py
│   ├── services/         # External integrations
│   │   ├── llm.py
│   │   ├── genai_client.py
│   │   ├── perplexity_search.py
│   │   └── prompt_loader.py
│   ├── domain/           # Shared state
│   │   └── state.py
│   ├── db/               # Database models
│   │   └── models.py
│   ├── graph.py          # LangGraph flow definition
│   ├── main.py           # CLI entry point
│   └── config.py         # Configuration
├── prompts/              # Editable LLM prompts
├── web/                  # FastAPI web interface
│   ├── main.py
│   ├── templates/
│   └── static/
├── scripts/              # Utility scripts
├── requirements.txt
└── README.md

docs.title: Research Agent
docs.text:
Discovers real-world inefficiencies using web search and LLM synthesis.

Flow: 1. Generates 6 strategic search queries from the topic 2. Executes Perplexity Search for discovery 3. Ranks and selects top 12 URLs 4. Sends URLs to Gemini with url_context tool for deep analysis 5. Extracts structured insights with evidence

State Updates: - research_notes - Raw Gemini response - research_sources - List of evidence with URLs - research_insights - Ranked insights with signals, evidence, tools - problem_statement, problem_root_cause, signals

docs.title: Problem Validation Agent
docs.text:
Human-in-the-loop review of discovered problems.

Features: - Auto-reject if no sources or generic sources - Captures human feedback for refinement - Configurable auto-approval via AUTO_APPROVE_PROBLEM=true

docs.title: Feasibility Analyzer Agent
docs.text:
Determines if research insights can be queried against the Neo4j schema.

Output: - Feasible insights with confidence level and query hints - Infeasible reports with gap analysis

docs.title: Query Generator Agent
docs.text:
Generates parameterized Cypher templates from feasible insights.

Output Format: 

{
  "tool_name": "PR Review Lead Time",
  "tool_description": "Analyzes pull request review delays...",
  "cypher_query": "MATCH (p:Person)-[:REVIEWED]->(pr:PullRequest)...",
  "parameters": [
    {"name": "orgId", "type": "string", "required": true}
  ],
  "reasoning": "...",
  "assumptions": ["..."]
}

docs.title: Human Validation Agent
docs.text: CLI-based Cypher query review with approval/rejection workflow.

docs.title: Query Executor Agent
docs.text:
Simulates or executes the generated Cypher query.

Modes: - Simulation (default) - Returns mock sensors - Real execution - Requires Neo4j driver configuration

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Default Description OPENAI_API_KEY Required OpenAI authentication LLM_MODEL gpt-4o-mini Default OpenAI model GEMINI_API_KEY Optional Gemini authentication GEMINI_MODEL gemini-3-flash-preview Gemini text model GEMINI_WEB_MODEL gemini-3-pro-preview Gemini web-enabled model GEMINI_WEB_TOOL google_search Tool: google_search, url_context, or both PREFER_GEMINI_WEB true Use Gemini with web tools for research PERPLEXITY_API_KEY Optional Perplexity search PREFER_PERPLEXITY_SEARCH true Use Perplexity for discovery PERPLEXITY_MAX_RESULTS 10 Results per query SIMULATE_EXECUTION true Simulate or execute queries AUTO_APPROVE_PROBLEM false Skip problem validation AUTO_APPROVE_CYPHER false Skip cypher validation MAX_PROBLEM_ATTEMPTS 3 Max research iterations MAX_CYPHER_ATTEMPTS 3 Max query generation iterations SHOW_PROGRESS true Show progress spinners REQUIRE_SOFTWARE_SOURCES true Reject generic sources

docs.title: Database Configuration
docs.text:
• DATABASE_HOST, DATABASE_USER, DATABASE_PASSWORD, DATABASE_NAME for MySQL
  • Supports RDS IAM authentication for AWS deployments

docs.title: Jira Integration
docs.text:
• JIRA_API_TOKEN - Required for epic creation
  • JIRA_PROJECT_KEY or JIRA_BOARD_URL - Target project
  • Scoped token mode: JIRA_CLOUD_ID
  • Basic token mode: JIRA_EMAIL + JIRA_BASE_URL

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt
python -m src.main "Problemas con lead time de cambios (DORA)"

docs.title: Web UI
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn web.main:app --reload --port 8000
Access at http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Web API Endpoints
docs.text: Endpoint Method Description /api/research POST Start research, returns session_id /api/status/:session_id GET Poll research progress /api/problem-validate POST Approve/reject problem /api/analyze-feasibility POST Run feasibility analysis /api/generate-cypher POST Generate Cypher query /api/validate-cypher POST Approve/reject Cypher /api/execute POST Run query (simulated) /api/save-insight POST Persist to database /api/upload-epic POST Create Jira epic

docs.title: Database Models
docs.text:
• SavedInsight - Stores feasible insights with feasibility analysis
  • SavedTool - Stores generated tools (Cypher, parameters, reasoning)
  • SavedPrompt - Stores generated prompts for Neo4j agents
  • SavedInfeasibleInsight - Gap reports for infeasible insights

docs.title: Extending the System
docs.text:
1. Real Query Execution - Replace simulation in query_executor.py with Neo4j driver
  2. Custom Research Engine - Alternative to Perplexity (e.g., custom scraper)
  3. Additional Validation - Add domain-specific validators between agents
  4. Advanced Persistence - Extend database models with custom fields
  5. Alternative LLMs - Add support for Claude, Cohere, etc. in llm.py
  6. Webhook Integration - Connect to n8n or Zapier for automated reactions

docs.title: Graph Visualization
docs.text:
Generate workflow diagrams: 

python scripts/render_graph.py

Outputs: - graph.mmd - Mermaid diagram - graph.png - PNG image (if supported)

docs.title: Leanmote Lambda Functions
docs.text: AWS Lambda packages that back the Leanmote platform. Each package is scoped to a specific concern and relies on the shared relational schema in database/.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Lambda repository contains 11 specialized serverless functions that handle authentication, API operations, data extraction, synchronization, and telemetry processing.

docs.title: API-AUTH
docs.text:
Custom API Gateway authorizer for token validation.

Purpose: - Validates database-backed API tokens against MySQL - Enforces organization scoping - Enriches requests with identity metadata (user ID, organization, role)

Security: - SHA256 hashing with static salt for token validation - Updates last_used_at timestamp for audit trails

Key File: lambda_function.py

docs.title: API-LEANMOTE
docs.text:
Main REST API entrypoint (v1.0).

Purpose: - Normalizes incoming requests and resolves routes to domain controllers - Queries MySQL backend and formats responses - Supports pagination, field filtering, timestamp comparisons, and sorting

Controllers (12 domains): - organizations_controller.py - Organization and member queries - teams_controller.py - Team management and membership - users_controller.py - User profile and relationships - tasks_controller.py - Task aggregation and worklog - boards_controller.py - Board and task queries - git_controller.py - Git repository queries - calendar_controller.py - Calendar and event queries - dashboard_views_controller.py - Dashboard and filter management - interactions_controller.py - Communication analytics - apps_controller.py - Application queries - admin_controller.py - API credential management - meta_controller.py - Health checks

Response Envelope: 

{
  "status": "success",
  "data": [...],
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "per_page": 50,
    "total": 100,
    "total_pages": 2,
    "sort": "created_at",
    "order": "desc"
  }
}

Query Features: - page, per_page - Pagination - sort, order - Sorting - fields - Field projection with dot-path support - Bracket operators: created_at[gte]=..., created_at[lte]=...

docs.title: DATA-EXTRACTOR
docs.text:
Event-driven collector for source-control integrations.

Purpose: - Dispatches operations to GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and generic Git routers - Tracks progress via database using ProcessTracker - Enqueues follow-up work on SQS FIFO queues

Supported Providers: - GitHub - Repositories, commits, PRs, reviews - GitLab - Projects, merge requests, discussions - Bitbucket - Repositories, commits, pull requests - Generic Git - Raw Git operations

Key Features: - Multi-provider router pattern - Webhook and direct invocation support - User ID mapping across platforms - Interaction recording (comments, approvals)

Directory Structure: 

DATA-EXTRACTOR/
├── lambda_function.py
├── routers/
│   ├── github_router.py
│   ├── gitlab_router.py
│   ├── bitbucket_router.py
│   └── git_router.py
├── functions/
│   ├── github/
│   ├── gitlab/
│   └── bitbucket/
└── services/

docs.title: EMAIL-SENDER
docs.text:
Utility for sending transactional emails.

Purpose: - Supports SES/SMTP backends - Reads credentials from AWS Secrets Manager - Validates payloads before sending - Reacts to SQS notifications

Key File: mail_sender.py

docs.title: SYNC-NEO4J
docs.text:
Orchestrates synchronization between Leanmote API and Neo4j Aura.

Purpose: - Prepares graph schema (constraints and indexes) - Runs generated Cypher queries - Intelligent scheduling (planning phase determines sync workload) - Window-based incremental syncing

Sync Domains: - Organizations, Teams, Users - Boards, Repositories - Commits, Pull Requests - Calendar Events - Person Metrics (WIP, throughput, review counts)

Key Features: - Scheduler determines sync workload (plan/execute phases) - Cursor tracking for resumable syncs - Support for full backfills and incremental updates

docs.title: COPILOT-EXTRACTOR
docs.text:
Extracts GitHub Copilot metrics.

Purpose: - Collects org-level, user-level, and billing seat data - Persists raw payloads to MySQL - Normalizes metrics for dashboards - Supports batch processing all organizations

Data Collected: - Daily org reports - User-level reports - Billing seats information

docs.title: SCIM-HANDLER
docs.text:
SCIM 2.0 Service Provider for user/group provisioning.

Purpose: - JumpCloud integration for user management - Served behind HTTP API Gateway at /scim/v2 - Bidirectional user/group synchronization

Endpoints: - POST/PATCH/DELETE /scim/v2/Users - POST/PATCH/DELETE /scim/v2/Groups

Key Files: 

SCIM-HANDLER/
├── scim_handler.py
├── lambda_function.py
├── services/
└── models/

docs.title: CLAUDE-CODE-OTEL-INGEST
docs.text:
Receives OpenTelemetry (OTLP) metrics and logs from Claude Code.

Purpose: - OTLP HTTP protocol v1.0 ingestion - Persists raw payloads to MySQL - Provides managed settings configuration for customers

Endpoints: - GET /health - Health check - POST /v1/logs - Log ingestion - POST /v1/metrics - Metrics ingestion

Key Features: - Token-based tenant resolution via SHA256 hash - Raw payload persistence for later analysis

docs.title: CLAUDE-CODE-NORMALIZE
docs.text:
Normalizes raw Claude Code OTLP data into fact tables.

Purpose: - Reads from claude_code_otel_ingest - Writes to claude_code_fact_metrics - Supports direct invocation and SQS batch processing

docs.title: METRICS-TEST
docs.text:
Provides comprehensive metrics APIs for dashboards.

Endpoints: - /get-data - General data retrieval - /context - Context information - /dora-metrics - DORA metrics - /flow-metrics - Flow metrics - /team-activity - Team activity - /project-financials - Project financials

Metrics Types: - DORA metrics - Flow metrics - Team activity - Interactions - Calendar - AI impact

docs.title: UPLOAD-DATA
docs.text:
Ingests raw app payloads via API Gateway.

Endpoint: POST /upload-data/{app_slug}

Features: - Token-based authentication - Stores payloads in upload_data_events table - Supports Claude Code (app_slug: "claude-code")

docs.title: Database Schema
docs.text: Total Tables: 33+

docs.title: Core Domain Tables
docs.text: Category Tables Organizations & Users organizations, users, user_organizations, timezones Teams teams, team_users, team_managers APIs & Credentials api_tokens, user_apps_credentials, user_apps_mapping, apps Boards & Tasks boards, board_tasks, board_task_statuses, board_task_statuses_log Git/Repositories repositories, repo_commits, repo_merge_requests Calendar calendars, calendar_events Interactions user_interactions, communication_channels Dashboards dashboards, dashboard_custom_filters, user_dashboard_filters Metrics & Insights metrics, insights, targets, variables OTEL & Telemetry claude_code_otel_ingest, claude_code_fact_metrics

docs.title: Key Relationships
docs.text:
• Users belong to multiple Organizations (many-to-many)
  • Teams contain Users (many-to-many)
  • Boards contain Tasks with hierarchical parent-child relationships
  • Repositories contain Commits and Merge Requests
  • API Tokens reference Users with expiry and usage tracking

docs.title: Security Features
docs.text:
1. Token Security
  2. SHA256 hashing with salt
  3. Token preview for user recognition
  4. Expiry tracking

  5. Usage auditing (last_used_at)

  6. Multi-tenancy

  7. Organization-scoped queries
  8. User membership validation

  9. Admin override capability

  10. Data Validation

  11. Bearer token extraction with strict parsing
  12. HTTP method validation

  13. Content-type enforcement

  14. Observability

  15. Structured JSON logging
  16. Request context injection
  17. Distributed tracing (trace_id in SQS messages)
  18. Process tracking with operation metadata

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: All Lambda functions are deployed on AWS with: - API Gateway for HTTP endpoints - SQS FIFO queues for ordered processing - Secrets Manager for credentials - RDS MySQL for data storage - Neo4j Aura for graph database

docs.title: Using app.leanmote.com
docs.text: This guide describes the main areas of the Leanmote web application and how teams use them in day-to-day operations.

docs.title: Main navigation areas
docs.text: Area Purpose Dashboards Track DORA, flow, and team execution metrics Integrations Connect and manage data sources Teams Organize members and ownership boundaries Settings Configure access, tokens, and organization-level preferences

docs.title: Dashboards
docs.text:
Dashboards are where most teams run delivery reviews.

Common usage patterns:

  • Weekly engineering review by team
  • Monthly leadership trend review
  • Incident recovery and delivery-risk follow-up

Recommended dashboard views:

  • Delivery health (DORA + flow)
  • Team execution view (throughput, cycle time, WIP)
  • Collaboration view (review participation and interaction patterns)

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text:
Leanmote supports cross-system analytics by syncing data from source control, planning, and collaboration tools.

Typical integration sequence:

  1. Connect source control
  2. Connect planning system
  3. Connect communication tools
  4. Validate team and repository/project mappings

docs.title: Team and access management
docs.text:
Keep your workspace accurate over time:

  • Maintain team ownership and membership
  • Review role permissions after org changes
  • Archive teams no longer in active delivery scope

docs.title: Operating model recommendation
docs.text:
Use Leanmote as part of a recurring cadence:

  • Weekly: team-level metrics review
  • Bi-weekly: bottleneck and workflow validation
  • Monthly: leadership KPI review and target adjustment

docs.title: Outcomes you should expect
docs.text:
• Faster identification of delivery bottlenecks
  • Better alignment between managers and executives
  • More consistent prioritization based on measurable trends

docs.title: Continue
docs.text:
• Metrics Methodology
  • Architecture Overview
  • API Overview

Source: lambda/API-LEANMOTE/lambda_function.py route table.

Method Path GET /admin/users/{user_id}/api-credentials POST /admin/users/{user_id}/api-credentials GET /apps GET /apps/{app_id} GET /boards GET /boards/{board_id}/tasks GET /calendar-events GET /calendar-events/{event_id} GET /calendars GET /calendars/{calendar_id} GET /dashboard-views GET /dashboard-views/{view_id} GET /dashboard-views/catalog GET /dashboard-views/custom-filters GET /git/apps GET /health GET /interactions GET /interactions/{interaction_id} GET /organizations GET /organizations/{organization_id} GET /organizations/{organization_id}/apps GET /organizations/{organization_id}/members GET /organizations/{organization_id}/teams GET /tasks GET /tasks/{task_id} GET /teams GET /teams/{team_id} GET /teams/{team_id}/interactions GET /teams/{team_id}/managers GET /teams/{team_id}/members GET /teams/{team_id}/members/{user_id} GET /teams/{team_id}/tasks GET /users GET /users/{user_id} GET /users/{user_id}/apps GET /users/{user_id}/apps/{app_id} GET /users/{user_id}/dashboard-views GET /users/{user_id}/dashboard-views/{view_id} GET /users/{user_id}/git/apps GET /users/{user_id}/interactions GET /users/{user_id}/organizations GET /users/{user_id}/teams GET /users/{user_id}/teams/{team_id}

Total endpoints: 43

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All Leanmote API requests require authentication using bearer tokens.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Leanmote uses token-based authentication for API access. Tokens are:

  • Generated through the web application
  • Hashed with SHA256 before storage
  • Can have optional expiration dates
  • Tracked for last usage (audit trail)

docs.title: Via Web Application
docs.text:
1. Log in to Leanmote at https://app.leanmote.com
  2. Navigate to Settings > API Tokens
  3. Click Generate New Token
  4. Optionally set an expiration date
  5. Copy the token immediately (it won't be shown again)

docs.title: Via Admin API
docs.text:
Administrators can generate tokens for users:

POST /v1/admin/users/{user_id}/api-credentials

docs.title: Using Your Token
docs.text:
Include the token in the Authorization header:

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
     https://api.leanmote.com/v1/organizations

docs.title: Header Format
docs.text:
Authorization: Bearer {token}

Important: The Bearer prefix is required. Tokens without this prefix will be rejected.

docs.title: Token Validation Process
docs.text:
When a request is received:

  1. Extract token from Authorization header
  2. Compute SHA256(token + salt)
  3. Query api_tokens table by hash
  4. Verify is_active = true
  5. Check expires_at (if set) has not passed
  6. Update last_used_at timestamp
  7. Enrich request with user context

docs.title: Token Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description token_hash string SHA256 hash (stored) token_preview string First 5 characters (for identification) is_active boolean Whether token is active expires_at datetime Optional expiration date last_used_at datetime Last usage timestamp created_at datetime Creation timestamp

docs.title: Missing Token
docs.text:
Status: 401 Unauthorized

{
  "status": "error",
  "error": {
    "code": "UNAUTHORIZED",
    "message": "Missing authentication token"
  }
}

docs.title: Invalid Token
docs.text:
Status: 401 Unauthorized

{
  "status": "error",
  "error": {
    "code": "UNAUTHORIZED",
    "message": "Invalid authentication token"
  }
}

docs.title: Expired Token
docs.text:
Status: 401 Unauthorized

{
  "status": "error",
  "error": {
    "code": "UNAUTHORIZED",
    "message": "Authentication token has expired"
  }
}

docs.title: Inactive Token
docs.text:
Status: 401 Unauthorized

{
  "status": "error",
  "error": {
    "code": "UNAUTHORIZED",
    "message": "Authentication token is inactive"
  }
}

docs.title: List User Tokens
docs.text:
Administrators can list tokens for a user:

GET /v1/admin/users/{user_id}/api-credentials

Response:

{
  "status": "success",
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "token_preview": "abc12",
      "is_active": true,
      "expires_at": "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z",
      "last_used_at": "2024-06-15T10:30:00Z",
      "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Create Token
docs.text:
POST /v1/admin/users/{user_id}/api-credentials

Request Body:

{
  "expires_at": "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z"
}

Response:

{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "token": "full-token-only-shown-once",
    "token_preview": "full-",
    "expires_at": "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z",
    "created_at": "2024-06-15T10:30:00Z"
  }
}

Important: The full token is only returned in the creation response. Store it securely.

docs.title: Revoke Token
docs.text: To deactivate a token, update its is_active status via the web application.

docs.title: Token Storage
docs.text:
• Never commit tokens to version control
  • Use environment variables or secrets managers
  • Rotate tokens periodically
  • Use separate tokens for different environments

docs.title: Token Scope
docs.text:
• Tokens inherit the user's permissions
  • Create dedicated service accounts for automated access
  • Use the minimum required permissions

docs.title: Monitoring
docs.text:
• Review last_used_at for unusual activity
  • Set expiration dates for temporary access
  • Revoke tokens when no longer needed

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
Authenticated requests are subject to rate limiting:

Limit Type Value Per Hour 1000 requests Per Minute 100 requests (burst)

Rate limit headers are included in responses:

X-RateLimit-Limit: 1000
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 950
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1609459200

docs.title: cURL
docs.text:
# Set token as environment variable
export LEANMOTE_TOKEN="your-api-token"

# Make authenticated request
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $LEANMOTE_TOKEN" \
     https://api.leanmote.com/v1/organizations

docs.title: Python
docs.text:
import requests
import os

token = os.environ.get('LEANMOTE_TOKEN')

response = requests.get(
    'https://api.leanmote.com/v1/organizations',
    headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}
)

data = response.json()

docs.title: JavaScript
docs.text:
const token = process.env.LEANMOTE_TOKEN;

const response = await fetch('https://api.leanmote.com/v1/organizations', {
  headers: {
    'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}`
  }
});

const data = await response.json();

docs.title: Organizations API
docs.text:
Primary endpoints are served by API-LEANMOTE. Full canonical lists:

  • API-LEANMOTE Endpoints
  • METRICS-TEST Endpoints

docs.title: API Overview
docs.text: The Leanmote API provides programmatic access to engineering metrics, teams, and dashboard data.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://api.leanmote.com/v1

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All API requests require authentication via bearer token:

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
     https://api.leanmote.com/v1/organizations

docs.title: Obtaining an API Token
docs.text:
1. Log in to Leanmote
  2. Navigate to Settings > API Tokens
  3. Click "Generate New Token"
  4. Copy and securely store the token (it won't be shown again)

docs.title: Token Security
docs.text:
• Tokens are hashed with SHA256 before storage
  • Tokens can have expiration dates
  • Last usage is tracked for auditing

docs.title: Response Format
docs.text: All responses follow a standard envelope:

docs.title: Success Response
docs.text:
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": [...],
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "per_page": 50,
    "total": 100,
    "total_pages": 2,
    "sort": "created_at",
    "order": "desc"
  }
}

docs.title: Error Response
docs.text:
{
  "status": "error",
  "error": {
    "code": "NOT_FOUND",
    "message": "Resource not found"
  }
}

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text:
Use query parameters for pagination:

Parameter Description Default page Page number 1 per_page Results per page (max 100) 50

Example:

GET /v1/teams?page=2&per_page=25

docs.title: Sorting
docs.text:
Control result ordering:

Parameter Description Default sort Field to sort by varies order Sort direction (asc, desc) desc

Example:

GET /v1/users?sort=name&order=asc

docs.title: Field Selection
docs.text:
Request specific fields:

GET /v1/users?fields=id,name,email

docs.title: Date Range Filtering
docs.text:
Filter by timestamps using bracket notation:

GET /v1/commits?created_at[gte]=2024-01-01&created_at[lte]=2024-12-31

Supported operators: - [eq] - Equal to - [gte] - Greater than or equal - [lte] - Less than or equal - [gt] - Greater than - [lt] - Less than

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
API requests are rate limited:

  • Standard: 1000 requests/hour
  • Burst: 100 requests/minute

Rate limit headers are included in responses:

X-RateLimit-Limit: 1000
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 950
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1609459200

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Code HTTP Status Description UNAUTHORIZED 401 Invalid or missing token FORBIDDEN 403 Insufficient permissions NOT_FOUND 404 Resource not found VALIDATION_ERROR 400 Invalid request parameters RATE_LIMITED 429 Too many requests SERVER_ERROR 500 Internal server error

docs.title: Organizations
docs.text:
• GET /organizations - List organizations
  • GET /organizations/{id} - Get organization
  • GET /organizations/{id}/members - List members
  • GET /organizations/{id}/teams - List teams

docs.title: Teams
docs.text:
• GET /teams - List teams
  • GET /teams/{id} - Get team
  • GET /teams/{id}/members - List team members
  • GET /teams/{id}/tasks - List team tasks

docs.title: Users
docs.text:
• GET /users - List users
  • GET /users/{id} - Get user
  • GET /users/{id}/teams - List user's teams
  • GET /users/{id}/interactions - List user interactions

docs.title: Git
docs.text:
• GET /git/apps - List git integrations
  • GET /git/apps/{id}/repositories - List repositories
  • GET /git/apps/{id}/repositories/{repo}/commits - List commits
  • GET /git/apps/{id}/repositories/{repo}/pull-requests - List PRs

docs.title: Dashboards
docs.text:
• GET /dashboard-views - List dashboards
  • GET /dashboard-views/{id} - Get dashboard
  • GET /dashboard-views/catalog - List available dashboard types

docs.title: SDKs and Libraries
docs.text: Official SDKs coming soon: - Python - JavaScript/TypeScript - Go

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text: Leanmote supports outgoing webhooks for real-time notifications. Configure webhooks in Settings > Integrations > Webhooks.

  • Organizations API - Organization endpoints
  • Teams API - Team endpoints
  • Users API - User endpoints

docs.title: Complete Endpoint Catalogs
docs.text:
• API-LEANMOTE Endpoints
  • METRICS-TEST Endpoints

docs.title: Teams API
docs.text: See API-LEANMOTE Endpoints for complete route definitions.

docs.title: Users API
docs.text: See API-LEANMOTE Endpoints for complete route definitions.

docs.title: Data Model
docs.text:
Leanmote data is centered on organizations, teams, users, tasks, interactions, repositories, and metric aggregates.

  • Organization is the top-level tenant boundary.
  • Teams and users are scoped by organization.
  • Tasks, boards, interactions, calendars, and git artifacts feed metrics pipelines.
  • API services read normalized tables and return domain envelopes with pagination metadata.

docs.title: Platform Overview
docs.text: Leanmote uses a product architecture designed for one purpose: turning engineering operational data into clear and actionable metrics.

docs.title: High-level architecture
docs.text:
flowchart LR
  A[Source Control\nGitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps] --> D[Leanmote Platform]
  B[Project Tracking\nJira, Linear, ClickUp] --> D
  C[Collaboration\nSlack and others] --> D
  D --> E[Dashboards in app.leanmote.com]
  D --> F[Public API]

docs.title: What this means for customers
docs.text:
• You connect systems your teams already use
  • Leanmote normalizes and correlates that data
  • Dashboards and API expose metrics for operational and strategic decisions

docs.title: Data lifecycle (public view)
docs.text:
1. Authorize integrations from the app
  2. Historical sync imports baseline data
  3. Incremental updates keep metrics current
  4. Dashboards and APIs return filtered, organization-scoped results

docs.title: Security and isolation principles
docs.text:
• Organization-scoped data boundaries
  • Role-based access for product users
  • Token-based API authentication
  • Auditable access and usage controls

docs.title: Reliability approach
docs.text:
• Automated sync retries for transient provider failures
  • Continuous monitoring of ingestion and metric freshness
  • Historical retention to support trend analysis over time

docs.title: Related docs
docs.text:
• Workspace Configuration
  • Metrics Methodology
  • API Overview

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• API access is token-based (Authorization header) and organization-scoped.
  • API-AUTH validates credentials and injects identity context for downstream authorization.
  • Sensitive DB access is handled in backend services only; no direct client DB access.
  • Production deployments should enforce TLS, least-privilege IAM, and secret rotation.

docs.title: Workspace Configuration
docs.text: This page covers the product-level configuration required for teams using app.leanmote.com.

docs.title: Organization setup checklist
docs.text:
• Organization profile and timezone are configured
  • Team structure reflects your real operating model
  • Role permissions are assigned (admin, manager, collaborator)
  • Default dashboard access is reviewed

docs.title: Integration configuration
docs.text:
For each connected provider:

  • Authorize with a service account that has stable access
  • Limit scope to relevant repositories/projects/boards
  • Validate webhook or sync permissions
  • Confirm repository/project ownership mapping

docs.title: Data quality controls
docs.text:
To keep metrics trustworthy:

  • Standardize workflow status mapping (for cycle/flow accuracy)
  • Confirm pull request and issue identities map to the correct users
  • Review inactive repositories and archive if no longer tracked
  • Keep team membership current

docs.title: Dashboard configuration best practices
docs.text:
• Define a default date window for operational reviews
  • Save team-level dashboard variants
  • Share executive views separately from team-operational views
  • Keep a standard KPI set across all engineering teams

docs.title: Access and governance
docs.text:
• Use least-privilege access for integrations
  • Rotate API tokens regularly
  • Audit new users and role changes monthly
  • Review public-share links and disable unused ones

docs.title: API access
docs.text:
If your team consumes Leanmote metrics programmatically:

  1. Generate API tokens in Settings > API Tokens
  2. Store tokens in your secrets manager
  3. Use API Overview and endpoint docs for integration

docs.title: Next
docs.text:
• Using app.leanmote.com
  • Architecture Overview
  • Metrics Methodology

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Leanmote is an engineering intelligence platform for teams that want measurable improvement in software delivery.

It unifies data from source control, project management, and communication tools to produce actionable dashboards for engineering managers, directors, and executives.

docs.title: Typical onboarding flow
docs.text:
1. Create or join your organization in app.leanmote.com
  2. Connect at least one source control tool and one planning tool
  3. Confirm data sync and repository/project mapping
  4. Open your first dashboard and validate baseline metrics
  5. Set goals and review trends weekly

docs.title: Who benefits most
docs.text:
• Engineering managers running multiple squads
  • VP/Head of Engineering teams tracking strategic delivery health
  • PMO or operations teams supporting engineering governance

docs.title: Metric families in Leanmote
docs.text:
• DORA: deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, change failure rate
  • Flow: throughput, cycle time, work in progress
  • Team execution: review participation, collaboration activity, delivery consistency

docs.title: Platform value in practice
docs.text:
• Replace fragmented reports with one shared metrics layer
  • Spot blockers early through trend and variance analysis
  • Make planning and staffing decisions with objective evidence

docs.title: Continue here
docs.text:
• Quick Start
  • Workspace Configuration
  • Using app.leanmote.com

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide gets a new organization from account creation to first insights quickly.

docs.title: 1. Access the platform
docs.text:
1. Sign in at https://app.leanmote.com
  2. Create an organization (or accept your invite)
  3. Confirm user role permissions for admins and managers

docs.title: 2. Connect your first tools
docs.text:
Recommended minimum setup:

  • One source control provider (GitHub, GitLab, or Azure DevOps)
  • One project/work tracker (Jira, Linear, ClickUp, or Azure Boards)

From the app:

  1. Open Settings > Integrations
  2. Select a provider and authorize access
  3. Choose repositories/projects/boards to sync

docs.title: 3. Validate data readiness
docs.text:
Before using dashboards, confirm:

  • Repositories and teams are mapped correctly
  • Historical sync completed successfully
  • Members are attached to the right teams

docs.title: 4. Open your first dashboard
docs.text:
1. Go to Dashboards
  2. Select a delivery-focused template
  3. Apply filters for date range and teams
  4. Review DORA and flow trend charts

docs.title: 5. Establish your baseline
docs.text:
Record your current baseline for:

  • Deployment frequency
  • Lead time for changes
  • Throughput
  • Cycle time

This gives your team a measurable starting point for improvement.

docs.title: 6. Start a weekly operating rhythm
docs.text:
• Weekly: review trend movement and outliers
  • Bi-weekly: validate workflow and team mappings
  • Monthly: review goals and compare against baseline

docs.title: Azure DevOps Integration
docs.text: Azure DevOps data can be mapped into boards, repositories, and delivery analytics views.

docs.title: GitHub Integration
docs.text: Leanmote ingests repositories, commits, pull requests, and review notes through the Git integration layer.

docs.title: GitLab Integration
docs.text: Leanmote ingests merge requests and commit activity to power delivery and collaboration metrics.

docs.title: Jira Integration
docs.text: Jira board and issue data contributes to flow, throughput, and planning-related metrics.

docs.title: Integrations Overview
docs.text: Leanmote integrates with 20+ development and collaboration tools to provide comprehensive engineering analytics.

docs.title: Source Control
docs.text: Platform OAuth Webhooks Data Collected GitHub Yes Yes Repos, commits, PRs, reviews, comments GitLab Yes Yes Projects, MRs, discussions, approvals Bitbucket Yes No Repos, commits, PRs Azure DevOps Yes Yes Repos, commits, PRs

docs.title: Project Management
docs.text: Platform OAuth Webhooks Data Collected Jira Yes Yes Projects, issues, sprints, history Linear Yes Yes Teams, issues, cycles ClickUp Yes Yes Spaces, lists, tasks Monday.com Yes Yes Boards, items, updates Notion Yes Yes Databases, pages Azure DevOps Boards Yes Yes Work items, sprints

docs.title: Communication
docs.text: Platform OAuth Webhooks Data Collected Slack Yes Yes Channels, messages, reactions Discord Yes No Servers, channels, messages

docs.title: Calendar
docs.text: Platform OAuth Webhooks Data Collected Google Calendar Yes No Events, attendees, duration

docs.title: AI Tools
docs.text: Platform OAuth API Data Collected GitHub Copilot N/A Yes Usage metrics, completions Claude Code N/A OTLP Sessions, tokens, commands

docs.title: OAuth Flow
docs.text:
1. User clicks "Connect {Platform}"
2. Redirect to provider's OAuth consent screen
3. User authorizes Leanmote
4. Provider redirects to callback URL with auth code
5. Leanmote exchanges code for access/refresh tokens
6. Tokens encrypted and stored in user_apps_credentials
7. Initial data sync triggered

docs.title: Data Synchronization
docs.text:
Pull-based (Cron): - Scheduled jobs fetch data from provider APIs - Incremental sync using last_update timestamps - Results stored in normalized tables

Push-based (Webhooks): - Provider sends events in real-time - Lambda validates and processes events - Immediate database updates

docs.title: Token Refresh
docs.text:
OAuth tokens are automatically refreshed:

  • Access tokens: Short-lived (1 hour typically)
  • Refresh tokens: Long-lived (30-90 days)
  • Automatic refresh before expiration
  • Failed refresh triggers re-authorization prompt

docs.title: Step 1: Navigate to Integrations
docs.text:
1. Log in to Leanmote
  2. Go to Settings > Integrations
  3. Find the platform you want to connect

docs.title: Step 2: Authorize Access
docs.text:
1. Click "Connect"
  2. Authorize Leanmote on the provider's consent screen
  3. Select the resources to share (repos, projects, etc.)
  4. Click "Authorize"

docs.title: Step 3: Configure Data Sources
docs.text:
1. After authorization, select which resources to track
  2. For GitHub: Choose repositories
  3. For Jira: Choose projects
  4. For Slack: Choose channels
  5. Click "Save Configuration"

docs.title: Step 4: Initial Sync
docs.text:
1. Leanmote queues initial data extraction
  2. Progress shown in Integration Status
  3. Historical data imported based on plan limits
  4. Ongoing sync begins automatically

docs.title: User Identity Mapping
docs.text:
Leanmote maps user identities across platforms:

Platform User Identifier GitHub username, email GitLab username, email Jira account ID, email Slack user ID, email

Mapping is stored in user_apps_mapping table.

docs.title: Interaction Recording
docs.text:
Cross-platform interactions are normalized:

Source Interaction Type PR comment Code review MR approval Code review Slack mention Communication Jira assignment Task assignment

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text:
Each provider has API rate limits:

Provider Rate Limit Reset GitHub 5000/hour Hourly GitLab 1000/hour Hourly Jira 1000/hour Hourly Slack Varies Per endpoint

Leanmote throttles requests to stay within limits.

docs.title: Connection Failed
docs.text:
1. Check OAuth credentials are correct
  2. Verify redirect URI matches exactly
  3. Ensure required scopes are granted
  4. Check provider status page

docs.title: Data Not Syncing
docs.text:
1. Check Integration Status for errors
  2. Verify credentials haven't expired
  3. Check cron job logs
  4. Ensure selected resources exist

docs.title: Missing Historical Data
docs.text:
1. Initial sync may take time
  2. Check plan limits for history depth
  3. Verify resource was active during period

docs.title: Token Storage
docs.text:
• Tokens encrypted at rest
  • Stored in user_apps_credentials table
  • Accessed only during sync operations

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
Each integration requests minimal required scopes:

Platform Scopes GitHub repo (read), user (read) GitLab read_api, read_user Jira read:jira-work, read:jira-user Slack channels:read, users:read

docs.title: Data Retention
docs.text:
• Active integrations: Data synced continuously
  • Disconnected integrations: Historical data retained
  • Account deletion: All data permanently removed

docs.title: Platform-Specific Guides
docs.text:
• GitHub Integration
  • GitLab Integration
  • Jira Integration
  • Slack Integration
  • Azure DevOps Integration

docs.title: Slack Integration
docs.text: Slack interactions feed collaboration and communication analytics in dashboards.

docs.title: Metrics Methodology
docs.text: This page explains how Leanmote measures engineering performance at a practical level.

docs.title: Measurement principles
docs.text:
• Use system-of-record events from connected tools
  • Preserve event timestamps to calculate lead and cycle durations
  • Keep metrics filterable by team, repository/project, and date window
  • Prioritize consistency over vanity metrics

docs.title: Deployment frequency
docs.text:
How often production deployments occur in the selected period.

Calculation:

deployment_frequency = total_successful_production_deployments / period_length
  • Numerator: production deployments marked successful in CI/CD or deployment events.
  • Denominator: explicit period length (day, week, month) for comparability.
  • Suggested views: per team, per service, and global.

Practical utility:

  • Detects delivery bottlenecks when frequency drops while backlog stays high.
  • Validates release process improvements (for example, smaller batch sizes or better automation).
  • Helps align release risk: higher frequency with stable quality usually means healthier flow.

docs.title: Lead time for changes
docs.text:
Elapsed time from code change to production deployment.

Calculation:

lead_time_for_change = deployed_at - commit_or_merge_at
  • Compute per change unit (commit, PR, or merge depending on source-of-truth choice).
  • Use median and P75/P90 in addition to average to avoid outlier distortion.
  • Optional decomposition:
  • coding_time = first_commit_at - issue_started_at
  • review_time = merged_at - opened_at
  • release_wait_time = deployed_at - merged_at

Practical utility:

  • Reveals where delivery delay is concentrated (review, test, or release queue).
  • Supports realistic SLA targets by percentile (for example, "80% under 2 days").
  • Prevents false confidence from fast-path averages.

docs.title: Mean time to recovery (MTTR)
docs.text:
Average time to restore normal operation after a production incident.

Calculation:

mttr = sum(resolved_at - incident_started_at) / incident_count
  • Include incidents tied to service degradation, outage, rollback, or urgent hotfix.
  • Track median MTTR alongside mean to separate frequent minor incidents from major outliers.
  • Keep severity labels (sev1, sev2, etc.) to analyze recovery quality by impact.

Practical utility:

  • Measures operational resilience and incident response readiness.
  • Highlights where runbooks, alert quality, or ownership clarity are weak.
  • Often improves after better observability and escalation paths.

docs.title: Change failure rate
docs.text:
Percentage of deployments that lead to incidents, rollbacks, or hotfixes.

Calculation:

change_failure_rate = (failed_or_degraded_deployments / total_deployments) * 100
  • Count a deployment as failed if it triggers incident creation, rollback, or emergency fix in the defined window.
  • Define a fixed failure attribution window (for example, 24h or 7d after deploy).
  • Track by service and team to isolate hotspots.

Practical utility:

  • Balances speed metrics to avoid rewarding unsafe throughput.
  • Shows where quality gates or test coverage are insufficient.
  • Enables risk-based release policy tuning.

docs.title: Throughput
docs.text:
Number of completed work items over a defined time period.

Calculation:

throughput = count(items_completed_in_period)
  • Completion should use explicit workflow transitions (for example, done state timestamp).
  • Segment by issue type (feature, bug, chore) to avoid mixed-signal totals.
  • Use rolling windows (7/14/30 days) to reduce calendar noise.

Practical utility:

  • Baseline team delivery capacity and trend direction.
  • Helps forecast near-term output when work mix is stable.
  • Useful leading indicator when paired with cycle-time distribution.

docs.title: Cycle time
docs.text:
Time from work start to completion.

Calculation:

cycle_time = completed_at - in_progress_at
  • Measure only active delivery period (not backlog wait time).
  • Track percentile distribution, not only mean.
  • Split by size class (S, M, L) or story points when available.

Practical utility:

  • Identifies queueing/coordination problems when cycle time rises without throughput gains.
  • Supports WIP policy design and pull-based workflow tuning.
  • Improves predictability for stakeholder commitments.

docs.title: Work in progress (WIP)
docs.text:
Number of items actively in progress during the selected window.

Calculation:

wip(t) = count(items where status in active_states at time t)
average_wip = avg(wip(t)) over period
  • Active states should be explicit (in_progress, in_review, qa, etc.).
  • Report both point-in-time WIP and period average WIP.
  • Use per-team and per-stream WIP caps for meaningful interpretation.

Practical utility:

  • Early warning for multitasking overload and context-switch cost.
  • High WIP with flat throughput typically indicates blocked flow.
  • Supports Little's Law reasoning: cycle_time ~ wip / throughput.

docs.title: Team execution and collaboration metrics
docs.text:
Leanmote also tracks operational behaviors that explain performance outcomes:

  • Review participation and review latency patterns
  • Activity distribution across teams and repositories
  • Execution consistency across planning and delivery systems

docs.title: Good interpretation habits
docs.text:
• Compare trends, not isolated single-week numbers
  • Segment by team to avoid mixed-signal conclusions
  • Investigate sharp metric changes with workflow context
  • Pair speed metrics with quality metrics to avoid local optimization

docs.title: Data quality and guardrails
docs.text:
• Keep event-source precedence explicit (for example, deployment system over manual notes).
  • Normalize timestamps to UTC and preserve original source timezone metadata.
  • Exclude known bad states from aggregates (duplicate deploy events, reopened incidents without new impact).
  • Publish metric definitions in change logs when formulas evolve to preserve historical comparability.

docs.title: Recommended review cadence
docs.text:
• Weekly: team-level operational review
  • Monthly: leadership trend and risk review
  • Quarterly: target reset using rolling historical performance

docs.text:

  • API Overview

docs.title: Docs Release Cadence and Publishing
docs.text: This runbook defines how Leanmote documentation changes are validated, released, and rolled back.

docs.title: Weekly release cadence
docs.text:
• Primary cadence: every Wednesday at 16:00 UTC.
  • Release owner: Docs Engineer on duty.
  • Scope for each release:
  • merged content updates from main
  • API reference updates
  • navigation/content quality fixes found during review

docs.title: CI quality gates
docs.text:
Every pull request and push to main must pass the docs quality workflow:

  • mkdocs build --strict to fail on warnings and broken references.
  • offline link integrity checks across docs markdown and MkDocs config.

Only changes that pass these checks should be merged.

docs.title: Publish workflow
docs.text:
Publishing to GitHub Pages is fully automated:

  1. Open a PR and wait for the docs quality workflow to pass.
  2. Merge into main.
  3. GitHub Actions runs the docs deploy workflow (Deploy docs).
  4. The build artifact is deployed to the github-pages environment.
  5. Validate key pages after deploy:
  6. home page
  7. changed page(s)
  8. search index behavior

docs.title: Rollback notes
docs.text:
If a bad docs release reaches production:

  1. Identify the last known good commit on main.
  2. Revert the bad commit(s) with a new commit (do not force-push).
  3. Push the revert commit to main.
  4. Confirm the deploy workflow completes successfully.
  5. Verify the site reflects the rollback and log the incident in the issue.

docs.title: Ownership checklist
docs.text:
• Keep the weekly release cadence unless a hotfix is required.
  • Keep CI workflows green before merge.
  • Record release-impacting exceptions in the related ticket.
